strengths: Clear selection of three specific, recognisable real-world objects with explicit contexts
Consistent links back to the IA prompt, showing how audience, medium and institutional pressures shape knowledge acceptance
Use of citations, quotations and concrete details to support most of the commentary
Comprehensive works cited section demonstrating engagement with diverse sources
A conclusion that effectively unifies the three objects around the promptâs theme
weaknesses: Analysis remains uneven and sometimes imprecise, with broad generalisations (e.g., majority of Russians) left unsupported
TOK concepts are referenced only implicitly; deeper conceptual reflection (bias, perspective, ways of knowing) is lacking
Repetition of the âaccepted or rejectedâ structure leads to a formulaic approach and limits depth
Instances of unsupported assertions and insufficient evaluation of source reliability
Introductory definitions are overly generic and administrative details distract from analytical content
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition fulfills the descriptors for the 5â6 band by identifying three specific objects, situating them in clear contexts, and explaining links to the IA prompt with supporting evidence. However, imprecision in claims, limited deployment of core TOK concepts and formulaic analysis prevent it from reaching the higher clarity and rigor required for band 7â8. A mark of 5 therefore accurately reflects solid achievement with notable areas for conceptual and evidential strengthening.

annotations.comment: The assertion that AI is uniformly accepted by students overlooks nuance (e.g., ethical concerns among learners). Incorporating empirical studies on student attitudes toward AI would provide depth.

Object 1 (PutinâCarlson interview) is a specific, recognisable real-world artifact. The choice effectively illustrates how context (political environment) influences knowledge acceptance in different audiences.

Although the conclusion ties examples together, it lacks explicit conceptual reflection. The student should synthesise core TOK concepts (e.g., bias, perspective) to demonstrate deeper analytical clarity.
The Works Cited section is comprehensive and demonstrates engagement with diverse sources, which supports the exhibitionâs academic integrity and evidential basis.
The conclusion restates how context affects knowledge acceptance across all three objects, reinforcing the IA prompt. This helps unify the exhibitionâs examples into a coherent argument.

The introductory definition of âknowledgeâ and âcontextâ is overly generic and descriptive. The student should integrate specific TOK concepts (e.g., ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks) to deepen the theoretical foundation and better connect to the IA prompt.
Acknowledging freedom of speech in the West as a contextual factor is a clear link to the IA prompt. This strengthens the exhibitionâs explanation of how context alters knowledge reception.
The exam session and word count details are administrative and do not contribute to the analysis. Focus remarks on content to strengthen the exhibition rather than formatting information.

The analysis recognises how propaganda and limited freedom of speech in Russia shape perception of Putinâs interview. This insight directly addresses the IA prompt by illustrating contextual pressure on knowledge acceptance.
The title and IA prompt are clearly presented, ensuring the reader immediately understands the exhibitionâs focus. This strong formatting supports coherence and meets TOK expectations for clarity.

Claiming that âeverything Putin said â¦ is accepted by a majority of Russiansâ is an overgeneralisation. The student should qualify this statement with empirical evidence or scholarly analysis to avoid unsupported assertions.

Citations of Vock and NOS Nieuws are mentioned but lack evaluation of source reliability. The student should assess each sourceâs credibility to strengthen justification for acceptance or rejection of knowledge.

The opening paragraph relies on a generic reference to âthe TOK course bookâ without precise citation. The student should provide full source details and engage more critically with literature to support claims about definitions.

The image is well-chosen, but the description could connect the visual symbolism to TOK concepts like authority and perspective. Explaining why the grand setting matters would enrich the link to context.

The discussion of Western rejection is descriptive. To deepen analysis, the student should reference specific epistemic communities or media critique studies to explain why different contexts lead to this dismissal.
Object 2 (ESS chapter) is relevant but presented without precise real-world context (e.g., lesson plan details, learner demographics). The student should specify setting to avoid generic references.

The analysis of student engagement is largely descriptive. Incorporating TOK concepts (e.g., shared vs personal knowledge, interest as a WOK) would move beyond âboring vs engagingâ and enrich justification.

The student effectively contrasts the textbook chapter with the documentary âBefore the Flood.â This comparison demonstrates how different mediums influence knowledge acceptance in varied audiences.

The claim regarding Leonardo DiCaprioâs influence lacks supporting data or theory. To strengthen this point, the student could reference audience research or the role of celebrity in shaping public understanding.
Object 3 (IB AI presentation) is a precise, real artifact from an educational institution. Its inclusion clearly shows how institutional context can influence acceptance of AI as a knowledge tool.

While student versus teacher perspectives are noted, the analysis remains descriptive. Integrating TOK vocabulary (e.g., authority, bias) would clarify why each groupâs context leads to acceptance or rejection.
âScottâ is cited in the text but missing from the Works Cited. Ensure all in-text citations correspond to full references to maintain academic rigour.

Repetition of the âaccepted or rejectedâ structure for each object leads to a formulaic analysis. Varying the analytical approach would enhance depth and precision in linking context to knowledge.
The bibliography shows inconsistency in formatting (e.g., italics, punctuation). Adhering strictly to a citation style would enhance professionalism and reliability of references.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
